description: Comprehensive BCI industry landscape tracker. Tracks companies, devices, security posture, funding, regulatory milestones, and publication trends. Part of the QIF project's mission to map the BCI security gap.

companies.name: Neuralink
companies.funding_note: ~$1.29-1.34B across 6 rounds per Sacra/Tracxn/PitchBook. Series E $650M (May 2025). Verified 2026-03-13.
companies.security_notes: No public security documentation. Implantable N1 chip with 1024 electrodes and wireless BLE telemetry. FDA IDE approved May 2023. First human implant Jan 2024. ~20 participants globally as of Dec 2025 (12 confirmed by Sep 2025, 7 in UK GB-PRIME) across PRIME (US), CAN-PRIME (Canada, Nov 2024), GB-PRIME (UK, Jul 2025). PMA application filed Nov 2024. Series E $650M closed Jun 2025. FDA Emergency Use Authorization for calibration software in acute locked-in syndrome (Apr 2025). Verified 2026-03-13 via CNBC, Neurapod, Neuralink updates.

companies.name: Synchron
companies.security_notes: No public security documentation. Endovascular Stentrode implanted via jugular vein. FDA pivotal trial IDE approved Jul 2023. 10 patients across US and Australia trials. First human 2019 (Melbourne, Australia); first US human Jul 2022. $200M Series D (Nov 2025) led by Double Point Ventures; IQT (In-Q-Tel, CIA/IC venture arm), Bezos Expeditions, QIA, and others participated. Next-gen transcatheter whole-brain interface in early development. AI decoding team established in NYC. Verified 2026-03-13.

companies.name: Blackrock Neurotech
companies.security_notes: No public security documentation. Utah Array is the most implanted cortical electrode in history (40+ patients since 2004). Used in BrainGate clinical trials. Tether invested $200M for majority stake (Apr 2024). MoveAgain BCI has FDA Breakthrough Device Designation. Launched Axon-R non-invasive AR-enabled BCI headset (partnership with Cognixion, May 2025).

companies.name: Precision Neuroscience
companies.security_notes: No public security documentation. Layer 7 thin-film electrode array with 1024 channels. Minimally invasive placement (sits on cortical surface). FDA 510(k) cleared Mar 30, 2025 (K242618) — first high-density cortical BCI to receive full regulatory clearance. 37 patients as of clearance filing. Clearance covers electrode array for up to 30-day implantation.

companies.name: Paradromics
companies.security_notes: No public security documentation. Connexus DDI: 421 channels per cortical module, up to 4 modules (1,684 channels max). The 65,000-channel figure from early roadmaps was a long-term aspiration, not current spec. First-in-human acute recording May 14, 2025 at University of Michigan (Dr. Matthew Willsey, 20-min procedure during epilepsy surgery, device implanted and removed). FDA IDE approved Nov 2025 for Connect-One Early Feasibility Study (first IDE for speech restoration with a fully implantable BCI). Chronic clinical study planned Q1 2026. Verified 2026-03-13 via CNBC, Paradromics PR, Michigan Medicine.

devices.name: Connexus Direct Data Interface
devices.units_deployed: 1 (first-in-human acute recording May 2025, University of Michigan)

companies.name: BrainGate
companies.security_notes: Research consortium (Brown, Stanford, MGH, Case Western). Uses Blackrock Utah Array. Academic research, not commercial. 15+ patients since 2004.

companies.name: Emotiv
companies.security_notes: Consumer EEG. Claims AES-128 encryption for data transfer. Cloud platform for data storage. No formal security audit published. Chile Supreme Court ruled against Emotiv in 2023 neurodata privacy case.

companies.name: NeuroSky
companies.security_notes: Consumer EEG with millions of units sold. MindWave single-channel headset. Minimal security documentation. Bluetooth LE.

companies.name: InteraXon (Muse)
companies.security_notes: Consumer meditation/sleep EEG. Claims Bluetooth encryption. Cloud platform with user accounts. No formal security audit published.

companies.name: OpenBCI
companies.security_notes: Open-source hardware and software. Community-auditable. No formal security certifications. Transparent by design.

companies.name: NextMind
companies.security_notes: Visual cortex BCI headband. Acquired by Snap 2022. Product discontinued. No security documentation was ever published.

companies.name: Kernel
companies.security_notes: Research-grade TD-fNIRS helmet. No published security documentation. Bryan Johnson invested $100M+ personally.

companies.name: CTRL-Labs
companies.security_notes: EMG wristband reading motor neuron signals. Acquired by Meta 2019 for ~$500M-$1B. Now Meta's neural interface team. No public BCI security docs.

companies.name: Merge Labs
companies.security_notes: Public Benefit Corporation spun out of Forest Neurotech (nonprofit FRO, est. 2023) in late 2025, publicly launched Jan 15, 2026. Co-founded by Sam Altman (OpenAI CEO), Alex Blania (Tools for Humanity CEO), Mikhail Shapiro (Caltech, HHMI), Sumner Norman (Forest Neurotech CEO), Tyson Aflalo (Forest Neurotech CSO), Sandro Herbig (Tools for Humanity). $252M seed led by OpenAI — largest BCI seed round ever. Also backed by Bain Capital, Interface Fund, Fifty Years, Gabe Newell (Valve). Core approach: functional ultrasound (fUS) imaging + genetically encoded molecular reporters (gas vesicle proteins) delivered via AAV gene therapy. ~100um spatial resolution, surface-to-deep-brain coverage claimed. No electrode penetration — ultrasound transducers sit on/near skull. Altman/OpenAI dual role has drawn conflict-of-interest scrutiny. Nature published scientific credibility assessment (2026). No device announced yet; Forest 1 (predecessor) is in NHS trial. No public security documentation. Gene therapy delivery vector (AAV) introduces novel TARA attack surface for genetic manipulation and viral vector integrity. OpenAI as investor raises AI-BCI convergence questions.

devices.name: Ultrasound Neural Interface (unnamed)
devices.note: Proposed non-implanted ultrasound-based neural interface. Miniaturized fUS transducers (Butterfly Network ultrasound-on-chip lineage) detect hemodynamic changes at ~100um resolution. Combined with AAV-delivered gas vesicle protein molecular reporters that reflect ultrasound ~38x better than normal tissue. Hardware sits on/near skull, not penetrating brain. No prototype publicly demonstrated. Bandwidth claims ('several orders of magnitude' over electrodes) are unvalidated. Pre-clinical stage — estimated 3-5 years to human trials, 10-15 years to commercial product.

devices.name: Forest 1
devices.note: Research-grade fUS scanner from Forest Neurotech (predecessor nonprofit). Smaller than a key fob. Enables functional brain imaging and neuromodulation from surface to deep structures. Currently in NCT06429735 NHS trial (30 patients with craniectomies, skull defect provides acoustic window). Funded by ARIA (UK) at GBP 6.5M. First human brain imaging using ultrasound-on-chip demonstrated at UCLA Neuro-ICU and Cedars-Sinai. Cognitive motor dissociation detected in comatose patients. Target applications: depression, mood disorders, addiction, OCD, epilepsy.

companies.name: AlterEgo (MIT Media Lab)
companies.security_notes: Academic research prototype. Subvocalization via jaw/face EMG. 92% word accuracy. No security analysis published.

companies.name: Neurosity
companies.security_notes: Claims on-device processing and encryption. SDK published. BLE communication. No formal security audit.

companies.name: BrainCo
companies.security_notes: Deployed EEG headbands in Chinese classrooms for attention monitoring. Major privacy concerns. Cloud data processing. Minimal security documentation.

companies.name: Nihon Kohden
companies.security_notes: Major medical device manufacturer. Subject to FDA cybersecurity requirements, IEC 62443, HIPAA. Limited public BCI-specific security documentation.

companies.name: Natus Medical (Integra LifeSciences)
companies.security_notes: CRITICAL: Xltek EEG has KNOWN CVEs (CVE-2017-2853, 2867, 2868, 2869). One of the ONLY neurotech companies with publicly disclosed vulnerabilities. ICS-CERT advisories published.

companies.name: g.tec medical engineering
companies.security_notes: Research-grade EEG/BCI systems used in academic research globally. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Cognixion
companies.security_notes: EEG+AR headset for ALS/cerebral palsy. No public security documentation.

companies.name: Cortera Neurotechnologies
companies.security_notes: Surface cortical electrodes. Pre-clinical. No public security documentation.

companies.name: MindMaze
companies.security_notes: VR/AR neural rehabilitation platform. Motion capture + EEG. No public security documentation.

companies.name: INBRAIN Neuroelectronics
companies.security_notes: Graphene-based invasive BCI. Smallest neural implant. EU government-backed. World's first human graphene-based BCI procedure completed Sep 2024 (during brain tumor resection). €4M PERTE Chip grant (Spain, May 2025). Series B $50M closed Oct 2024. No public security documentation.

companies.name: Neurable
companies.security_notes: EEG-enabled headphones for focus tracking. Consumer BCI in headphone form factor. No public security documentation.

companies.name: Arctop
companies.security_notes: Software platform for decoding brain signals from consumer EEG. Cloud-based neural analytics. No public security documentation.

devices.name: Arctop Neural Decode SDK
devices.note: Software platform, works with third-party EEG hardware

companies.name: Science Corporation
companies.security_notes: Founded by Max Hodak (Neuralink co-founder/president, left 2021) and Alan Mardinly PhD (CSO, former Neuralink). Three device programs spanning retinal to cortical. PRIMA: 378-pixel subretinal photovoltaic implant, wireless NIR power, write-capable stimulation — elevated TARA attack surface for injection/manipulation. Science Eye: 8,192-pixel supra-retinal micro-LED + optogenetic gene therapy (deferred until post-PRIMA approval). Biohybrid: cortical surface interface using stem cell-derived living neurons in silicon scaffold (~90,000 microwells, early rodent PoC). NEJM published PRIMAvera pivotal results Oct 2025 (DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa2501396). CE mark + FDA submissions June 2025. IQT (CIA/IC venture arm) participated in Series C — national security implications noted. No public security documentation for any device program.

devices.name: PRIMA Retinal Implant
devices.note: 2mm x 2mm x 30um crystalline silicon photovoltaic array, 378 pixels at 100um each. Implanted subretinally for geographic atrophy (dry AMD). Wireless power via near-infrared projection glasses + pocket processor. Originally developed by Prof. Daniel Palanker (Stanford); commercialized by Pixium Vision (acquired April 2024 from Paris Commercial Court liquidation). PRIMAvera trial: 38 patients, 17 sites, 5 EU countries — mean +25.5 ETDRS letters at 12 months, 80% achieved logMAR 0.2+ improvement. NEJM Oct 2025 (DOI: 10.1056/NEJMoa2501396, PMID: 41124203). CE mark application June 2025 (Germany first market, decision expected mid-2026). FDA application June 2025 (decision pending). Clinical trial IDs: NCT04676854 (PRIMAvera EU pivotal), NCT03392324 (US feasibility, UPMC Pittsburgh).

devices.name: Science Eye
devices.note: 30um thin flexible wireless micro-LED display (FlexLED) applied to retinal surface + optogenetic gene therapy to make retinal ganglion cells light-sensitive. 8,192 pixels (~8x iPhone 13 Super Retina density). Targets retinitis pigmentosa and dry AMD where photoreceptors are dead but RGCs survive. Display moves with the eye (constant pixel-to-cell mapping). Wireless power coil under conjunctiva. Deferred until PRIMA receives first regulatory approval. bioRxiv preprint Feb 2023 (doi:10.1101/2023.01.31.526482v2).

devices.name: Biohybrid Neural Interface
devices.note: Cortical surface interface using stem cell-derived living neurons embedded in silicon scaffold with ~90,000 microwells (~5mm square). Neurons grow into brain tissue forming biological synaptic connections — avoids penetrating electrodes. LEDs for optical stimulation, electrical contacts for recording. Potential for ~1 billion synapses with host cortex. Rodent behavior guidance demonstrated (bioRxiv Nov 2024, Brown et al.). Led by Alan Mardinly (CSO) and Yifan Kong. Estimated decade+ before human trials. Future applications: cognition and mobility restoration.

companies.name: Cortigent
companies.security_notes: Invasive cortical visual prosthesis with 60-electrode subdural occipital grid. Write-capable stimulation device with no published security documentation.

companies.name: Pixium Vision
companies.security_notes: Acquired by Science Corporation April 2024. PRIMA IP transferred. No published security documentation prior to acquisition.

companies.name: GenSight Biologics
companies.security_notes: AAV optogenetic gene therapy with light goggles for vision restoration. Novel modality combining gene therapy and external light device. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Nano Retina
companies.security_notes: Wireless inductive subretinal microchip. Wireless power transfer creates RF attack surface. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Subsense
companies.security_notes: Inhalable nanoparticles magnetically steered to brain regions. Novel delivery mechanism with unique biosecurity implications. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Motif Neurotech
companies.security_notes: DOT miniature wireless cortical microstimulator for depression. Write-capable implant with wireless interface. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Axoft
companies.security_notes: Ultra-soft compliant polymer neural implant. FDA Breakthrough Device designation. Novel materials approach. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Forest Neurotech
companies.security_notes: Low-intensity focused ultrasound (LIFU) with genetic sensitization. Write-capable neuromodulation via ultrasound. No published security documentation.

devices.name: Forest 1 Ultrasound BCI
devices.note: 30-patient NHS trial at BHR University Hospitals, funded by ARIA (£6.5M). Patients with craniectomies (skull defect provides acoustic window). First human brain imaging using ultrasound-on-chip demonstrated at UCLA Neuro-ICU and Cedars-Sinai. Spun out commercially as Merge Labs (Jan 2026).

companies.name: CorTec GmbH
companies.security_notes: Fully implantable wireless closed-loop BCI. First human implant July 2025. Bidirectional read/write with wireless telemetry. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Medtronic DBS
companies.security_notes: Closed-loop adaptive DBS with BrainSense technology. FDA approved Feb 2025. Public company (NYSE:MDT). Major medical device manufacturer with no BCI-specific published security posture.

companies.name: Abbott Neuromodulation
companies.security_notes: Closed-loop DBS with remote programming capability. FDA approved Jan 2024. Public company (NYSE:ABT). Remote programming introduces network attack surface. No BCI-specific published security posture.

companies.name: Boston Scientific Neuromodulation
companies.security_notes: Directional-lead DBS with 16-contact electrode. Public company (NYSE:BSX). No BCI-specific published security posture.

companies.name: Saluda Medical
companies.security_notes: Closed-loop SCS with ECAP (evoked compound action potential) feedback control. First objective closed-loop pain management. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Galvani Bioelectronics
companies.security_notes: Implanted splenic nerve stimulation for rheumatoid arthritis. GSK (55%) + Verily (45%) joint venture. £540M GBP (~$715M USD at 2016 rate) committed over 7 years, contingent on milestone achievement. No published security documentation.

companies.name: ONWARD Medical
companies.security_notes: ARC-BCI system combining WIMAGINE cortical implant with epidural spinal cord stimulation. FDA Breakthrough Device. Dual-implant architecture with wireless cortical recording. No published security documentation.

companies.name: NeuroXess
companies.security_notes: Flexible intracortical electrode with implanted battery. 54 human implants by early 2026. Fully implanted system with no published security documentation.

companies.name: Neuracle Technology
companies.security_notes: EEG-based BCI and ECoG research systems. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Zhiran Medical
companies.security_notes: Flexible high-throughput intracortical electrodes. HongShan Capital backed. No published security documentation.

companies.name: BrainsWay
companies.security_notes: Deep TMS with H-coil technology. Public company (NASDAQ+TASE). 100K+ patients treated, 3 FDA indications. Write-capable neuromodulation with no BCI-specific published security posture.

companies.name: Alpha Omega Engineering
companies.security_notes: Intraoperative MER with ML-assisted DBS targeting. First FDA/CE cleared ML algorithm for DBS electrode placement. ML decision-making in neurosurgery with no published security posture.

companies.name: Neuroelectrics
companies.security_notes: Multichannel HD-tDCS and EEG platform (Starstim/Enobio). Combined read (EEG) and write (tCS) capability. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Flow Neuroscience
companies.security_notes: At-home tDCS headset with app-guided CBT. FDA approved Dec 2025. Consumer device with app connectivity. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Openwater
companies.security_notes: Low-intensity focused ultrasound (LIFU) platform. Founded by Mary Lou Jepsen. Write-capable neuromodulation via ultrasound. No published security documentation.

companies.name: Soterix Medical
companies.security_notes: HD-tDCS, tACS, and tRNS neurostimulation systems. Write-capable multi-modality stimulation. No published security documentation.

companies.name: NeuroPace
companies.security_notes: RNS System is a read-write closed-loop cortical BCI — continuously reads ECoG and delivers responsive stimulation. FDA PMA approved Nov 2013 (P100026) for drug-resistant focal epilepsy. Commercially deployed with thousands of patients — the most mature closed-loop BCI in clinical use. FDA Breakthrough Device Designation for idiopathic generalized epilepsy expansion. Direct TARA attack surface: closed-loop read-write device with stimulation capability. No public security documentation.

devices.name: RNS System
devices.note: Responsive neurostimulation for drug-resistant focal epilepsy. Continuously monitors ECoG, detects seizure onset patterns, delivers targeted stimulation. FDA PMA P100026 (Nov 2013). Up to 2 epileptogenic foci. The most commercially deployed closed-loop cortical BCI.

companies.name: LivaNova
companies.security_notes: VNS Therapy is the longest-deployed neurostimulation system in history. 100,000+ patients implanted since FDA approval in 1997. Semi-invasive: pulse generator implanted in chest, lead wrapped around left vagus nerve. Approved for epilepsy (1997) and treatment-resistant depression (2005). Nasdaq: LIVN (public company, formed from Sorin Group + Cyberonics merger 2015). No public security documentation. Peripheral nerve modality — distinct TARA attack surface from cortical devices.

devices.name: VNS Therapy System
devices.note: Vagus nerve stimulation for epilepsy (1997) and treatment-resistant depression (2005). Pulse generator implanted subclavicularly, lead wrapped around left vagus nerve. Longest-deployed neurostimulation system in clinical history.

companies.name: Cognito Therapeutics
companies.security_notes: MIT spinout. Sensory gamma entrainment (40Hz visual + auditory stimulation) for Alzheimer's disease. $105M Series C (Mar 2026). HOPE Study: largest medical device trial focused solely on Alzheimer's (70 sites, randomized, sham-controlled). Non-invasive neuromodulation via entrainment — consumer-adjacent attack surface. No public security documentation.

devices.name: Spectris
devices.note: Combined visual + auditory 40Hz gamma frequency stimulation headset. HOPE Study is the largest AD-focused device trial. Based on MIT Li-Huei Tsai lab research on gamma entrainment reducing amyloid plaques in mouse models.

companies.name: Inner Cosmos
companies.security_notes: Minimally invasive cortical implant for treatment-resistant depression. Electrodes placed in skull bone, not penetrating cortex — novel minimally invasive category between EEG and deep cortical implants. FDA Early Feasibility Study completed Nov 2025, all safety/feasibility endpoints met. Proceeding to pivotal trial (US + Australia). Psychiatric indication raises distinct neuroethics considerations for TARA coverage of psychiatric targeting. No public security documentation.

devices.name: Cortical Implant for Depression
devices.note: Minimally invasive: electrodes in skull bone targeting cortex without penetrating brain tissue. EFS completed Nov 2025 with all endpoints met. Pivotal trial planned for US and Australia.

companies.name: Magnus Medical
companies.security_notes: Stanford spinout. SAINT Neuromodulation System (accelerated TMS with fMRI-guided individualized targeting). FDA 510(k) cleared Sep 2022 (K220177). Breakthrough Device Designation. Commercial launch Apr 2024. $64M venture round Mar 2025 + $23.7M Series B-II May 2025. Precision targeting via fMRI raises distinct threat considerations (session manipulation, targeting drift). No public security documentation.

devices.name: SAINT Neuromodulation System
devices.note: Accelerated TMS protocol (SAINT) with fMRI-guided individualized targeting for treatment-resistant depression. FDA 510(k) K220177 cleared Sep 2022. Based on Stanford SAINT protocol research. Commercial launch April 2024.

companies.name: Rune Labs
companies.security_notes: Software layer on top of implanted DBS devices. StrivePD is FDA-cleared 510(k) software integrating Apple Watch data with Medtronic Percept PC DBS neural sensing data. Represents the neural data pipeline/exfiltration TARA attack surface — not the implant itself, but the data pathway from implant to cloud. $12M Series A (Jan 2024, Nexus NeuroTech lead). No public security documentation.

devices.name: StrivePD
devices.note: FDA-cleared software ecosystem integrating Apple Watch movement data with Medtronic Percept PC DBS neural sensing. Digital biomarkers for Parkinson's disease management. The data pipeline from implanted DBS to cloud analytics represents a distinct TARA attack surface for neural data exfiltration.

adjacent_tech.name: Apple
adjacent_tech.note: NOT a BCI. Neural-adjacent: FDA-cleared hearing test, head tracking, health sensors. Included for sensor convergence trend. Multiple BCI patents filed. Industry-leading security (Secure Enclave, E2E encryption).
adjacent_tech.relevance: Sensor convergence: consumer earbuds adding biosensing capabilities that may eventually include EEG. Apple has filed BCI-related patents.

major_funding_rounds.note: Tether became majority stakeholder. Crypto-to-BCI investment.
Largest seed round in BCI history. Co-founded by Sam Altman. $252M = ~3% of OpenAI's estimated ~$8.5B annual burn rate.
Largest BCI raise outside USA. HK IPO filed Jan 2026. Total funding: $370M.

all_investors: Hangzhou Science and Technology Innovation Fund

major_funding_rounds.note: Bryan Johnson invested $54M initially, eventually committing $100M+ personally.
Amount undisclosed. NEOM = Saudi sovereign wealth fund subsidiary. Total funding ~$127M across 49 investors.
Total funding: $82.8M from 16 investors. Predominantly European institutional capital.
First BCI unicorn. DiCaprio also became board advisor.
Total funding: $29M across 13 investors. Mix of corporate venture + health systems.

cross_portfolio_vcs.total_bci_exposure_note: Lead in Neuralink D + co-lead E ($930M+ rounds). Early CTRL-Labs before Meta acquisition.
Lead in Synchron B. Participated in Kernel C. Multi-modality bet: endovascular + fNIRS.
Lead in Synchron C ($75M). Also lead in Science Corporation ($160M, visual cortex BCI, not in this tracker). Deep biotech specialist.
Lead in Paradromics Seed + A ($70M). Early Cognixion. Deep tech focus.
Early Neuralink C + C extension. CTRL-Labs B (pre-Meta acquisition). Both portfolio companies were early-stage bets.
Series A through Series C. Repeat investor across 3 rounds.
Series A through Series C. Repeat investor across 3 rounds.
Neuralink C extension through Series E. Multi-round repeat investor.
Lead in Neuralink C extension ($205M). Repeat through D and E. One of Neuralink's most consistent backers.
Neuralink C extension through Series E. Multi-round. Known Musk ecosystem investor (also in Tesla, SpaceX).
Invested in Cognixion (accessibility BCI) and CTRL-Labs (pre-Meta acquisition).
Synchron C and D. Repeat investor.
Series A through B-1. Canadian pension fund with 3-round repeat commitment.

sovereign_wealth_funds.note: QIA invested in both the #1 and #2 invasive/semi-invasive BCI companies in the same year (2025). Dual-bet hedging strategy.
Saudi sovereign fund. Linked to NEOM smart city project. Strategic interest in neurotechnology for future city infrastructure.
UAE sovereign wealth fund. First BCI investment identified in 2023.
Australian government backing for Australian-founded BCI company (Synchron was founded in Melbourne).

big_tech_corporate_venture.note: Led largest BCI seed round ever. Sam Altman co-founded Merge Labs. Conflict of interest questions raised (TechCrunch). Also: Sam Altman personally invested in Neuralink Series C.
Invested in Neuralink C/C-extension and CTRL-Labs B. Alphabet also has internal neural interface research.
Acquired CTRL-Labs for $500M-$1B (2019). Now internal Meta neural interface team. Not a VC investor, but largest BCI acquisition to date.
Acquired NextMind for ~$13M (2022). Product discontinued post-acquisition. Talent acquisition.
Invested in accessibility-focused BCI (Cognixion) and peripheral EMG (CTRL-Labs pre-Meta).
Impact investing arm. Accessibility-focused BCI bet.

pe_firms.note: Participated in Merge Labs $252M seed. Bain Capital is one of the largest PE firms globally.
Co-led BrainCo's CNY 2B ($286M) round. One of China's largest PE/VC firms.
Billionaire hedge fund manager's family office. Precision Series C.
Stablecoin issuer invested $200M to become majority stakeholder. Unprecedented crypto-to-BCI crossover.
Led MindMaze's $110M Seed+A at $1B valuation. Indian industrial conglomerate.

intelligence_agencies_defense.note: US intelligence community's venture capital arm invested in Synchron's $200M Series D (2025).
US Department of Defense participated in Synchron's earliest funding round (2017).
Not equity investment. DARPA NESD grants ($65M program) funded Paradromics, Brown, Columbia, Berkeley.

notable_individual_investors.name: Sam Altman
notable_individual_investors.note: Invested personally in Neuralink C. Then co-founded Merge Labs and had OpenAI lead the $252M seed. Deepest personal BCI commitment of any tech CEO.

notable_individual_investors.name: Jeff Bezos
notable_individual_investors.note: Via Bezos Expeditions. Series C + D repeat investor.

notable_individual_investors.name: Bill Gates
notable_individual_investors.note: Via Gates Frontier. Series C investor.

notable_individual_investors.name: Elon Musk
notable_individual_investors.note: Founder. Personal capital in Seed + early rounds.

notable_individual_investors.name: Peter Thiel
notable_individual_investors.note: Via Founders Fund (co-founder). Led Series D.

notable_individual_investors.name: Leonardo DiCaprio
notable_individual_investors.note: Investor + board advisor. Series A era.

notable_individual_investors.name: Gabe Newell
notable_individual_investors.note: Valve co-founder. Participated in Merge Labs seed. Valve has been researching BCI for gaming since 2021.

notable_individual_investors.name: Stanley Druckenmiller
notable_individual_investors.note: Via Duquesne Family Office. Series C.

key_patterns: Qatar Investment Authority (QIA) is the ONLY investor confirmed in both Neuralink AND Synchron, hedging across the two leading implantable BCI approaches.
3 sovereign wealth funds active in BCI: QIA (Qatar), NEOM/PIF (Saudi Arabia), Mubadala (UAE). All Gulf states. No European or Asian sovereign funds identified.
Big Tech involvement is primarily through acquisitions (Meta $500M-$1B, Snap $13M) rather than venture rounds. Exception: OpenAI's $252M into Merge Labs.
US intelligence community (IQT/In-Q-Tel) invested in Synchron Series D (2025). Combined with DoD in Series A (2017), Synchron has the deepest government/defense funding ties of any BCI startup.
Sam Altman has the most complex BCI entanglement: personal Neuralink investor, Merge Labs co-founder, OpenAI leading the investment. Raises governance questions.
Tether ($200M into Blackrock Neurotech) represents the first major crypto-to-BCI crossover investment.
No traditional institutional investors (Vanguard, BlackRock Asset Management, Fidelity) identified in BCI venture rounds. BCI remains too early-stage for institutional capital.

Chinese BCI funding ($370M BrainCo) flows through different channels: government innovation funds (Hangzhou Capital), industrial conglomerates (Lens Technology), gaming companies (37 Interactive), and China-focused PE (IDG Capital).
European BCI funding (INBRAIN $82.8M) relies heavily on EU institutional mechanisms: European Investment Bank, EIC Fund, government programs (CDTI, Fond ICO Next Tech). Less private VC.
Merge Labs' $252M seed at $850M valuation is the largest seed round in BCI history and represents ~3% of OpenAI's estimated annual operating costs.
PE firms are entering BCI: Bain Capital (Merge Labs), IDG Capital (BrainCo), General Equity Holdings (Precision). The asset class is graduating from pure VC.
OMERS Ventures (Canadian pension fund) is the only pension fund identified investing directly in BCI companies (InteraXon/Muse).

government_grants.note: $278M cut in 2025-2026 under restructuring. Future uncertain.
High-res implantable BCI. Funded Paradromics, Brown, Columbia, Berkeley.
EUR 4M grant for graphene BCI advancement

security_gap.note: No BCI company has published a security audit, bug bounty program, or dedicated CISO hire as of Feb 2026

industry_benchmarks.source: IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024, Gartner, IANS Research

key_findings: 700% increase in FDA cybersecurity deficiency letters since Oct 2023 (MedCrypt)
Yale 2025 study: BCI security practices 'remain uneven and continue to evolve'
GAO-25-106952: consumer BCIs face essentially no federal privacy or security laws
No BCI company lists dedicated cybersecurity roles publicly
Neuralink filed 1 security patent (out-of-band pairing). One patent is not a security program.

key_sources.title: GAO Brain Signal Device Privacy Report
Schroder et al. Cyber Risks to Next-Gen BCIs
Bernal et al. Eight Reasons to Prioritize BCI Cybersecurity (ACM)
IBM Cost of a Data Breach 2024 (Healthcare)

policy_timeline.event: Chile constitutional neurorights amendment (Art. 19 §1)
policy_timeline.note: First country to constitutionally protect neurorights

policy_timeline.event: FDORA Section 3306: NIH neurotechnology ethics consideration
policy_timeline.note: Requires NIH to consider neurotech ethics, but no binding BCI rules

policy_timeline.event: FDORA Section 524B: mandatory device cybersecurity
FDA updated premarket cybersecurity guidance
Latin American Parliament Model Law on Neurorights
policy_timeline.note: Model legislation for member states; not binding

policy_timeline.event: OECD neurotechnology recommendation
Colorado SB 24-058: neural data as sensitive data under state privacy law
China MoST BCI Ethics Guidelines
policy_timeline.note: National BCI-specific ethics guidelines. Covers all BCI research and deployment. Ministry-level binding on state-funded research.

policy_timeline.event: EU AI Act enacted (Regulation 2024/1689)
policy_timeline.note: Classifies neurotech as high-risk AI. Phased implementation 2025-2026.

policy_timeline.event: UN Human Rights Council advisory mandate on neurotechnology
policy_timeline.note: HRC advisory committee mandate to study neurotechnology and human rights

policy_timeline.event: California AB 1008: neural data under CCPA
Montana SB 214: neural data as biometric data
Minnesota HF 1370: neural data as sensitive biometric data
Global Privacy Assembly 46th Conference: neural data processing resolution
policy_timeline.note: Non-binding resolution on neural data processing standards

policy_timeline.event: EU AI Act prohibited practices take effect
MIND Act introduced: Mental Integrity in the Digital Age
policy_timeline.note: Not enacted. Still in committee. Would be first US federal BCI-specific legislation.

policy_timeline.event: Montana neural data privacy law effective
Connecticut neural data privacy law
FDA updated postmarket cybersecurity guidance
EU AI Act high-risk requirements take effect
UNESCO neurotechnology ethics recommendation
California SB 1223 neural data protections effective

data_sources: FDA device databases (510(k), PMA, Breakthrough, IDE)
Grand View Research, Fortune Business Insights
limitations: Funding amounts are approximate. Many companies do not disclose exact figures.
Units deployed are estimates. Most BCI companies do not publish sales data.
PubMed publication counts are approximate order-of-magnitude estimates.
TARA technique IDs mapped by category, not individually validated per company.
Security posture based on public information only. Companies may have unpublished measures.
Market size estimates vary between research firms.
